\begin{table*}[t]
\centering\small
\caption{Fixed-test results (185 images from 28 subjects, all metrics on the same $640\times640$ grid). Brackets are 95\% subject-level cluster-bootstrap intervals ($B{=}4000$, resampling subjects). SegFormer operating points are validation-selected thresholds; YOLO uses Ultralytics' native \texttt{argmax} (no tunable threshold). Throughput is fp32 raw-forward on one A100. \emph{The intervals are wide: at 28 subjects most between-model accuracy differences here are not resolvable} (Table~\ref{tab:paired}).}
\label{tab:main}
\begin{tabular}{llrrccrr}
\toprule
Model & Training & Params & $\tau$ & Med.\ Dice [95\% CI] & Mean Dice [95\% CI] & Miss\% & FPS \\

SegFormer-B0 (direct) & direct & 3.71M & 0.60 & 0.811 [0.765, 0.850] & 0.775 [0.747, 0.805] & 0.54 [0.00, 1.80] & 96.0 \\
SegFormer-B0 (distilled) & distilled & 3.71M & 0.55 & 0.828 [0.803, 0.856] & 0.787 [0.757, 0.816] & 0.54 [0.00, 1.80] & 95.6 \\

\begin{table*}[t]
\centering\small
\caption{\textbf{Paired} subject-level cluster bootstrap ($B{=}4000$) for each comparison the paper makes. Both models are evaluated on the same 185 images, so differences are paired; each bootstrap iteration resamples subjects once and scores both models on that resample. Intervals excluding zero are marked \textbf{sig}. Only the \emph{failure-mode} differences are resolvable at this sample size---no Dice difference is.}
\label{tab:paired}
\begin{tabular}{lccc}
\toprule
Comparison & $\Delta$ Med.\ Dice [95\% CI] & $\Delta$ Mean Dice [95\% CI] & $\Delta$ Miss\% [95\% CI] \\
\midrule
Distillation: B0-distilled $-$ B0-direct & +0.018 [-0.004, +0.044] & +0.012 [-0.006, +0.032] & +0.00 [+0.00, +0.00] \\
Student $-$ teacher: B0-distilled $-$ B2 & +0.001 [-0.027, +0.042] & +0.021 [-0.016, +0.060] & +0.54 [+0.00, +1.80] \\
YOLO-direct $-$ B0-distilled & +0.020 [-0.025, +0.041] & -0.052 [-0.118, +0.010] & \textbf{+9.19 [+1.83, +17.54]}\,sig \\
YOLO distillation: distilled $-$ direct & -0.005 [-0.032, +0.013] & +0.006 [-0.019, +0.030] & \textbf{-2.70 [-5.03, -0.80]}\,sig \\
\bottomrule
\end{tabular}
\end{table*}

\begin{table}[t]
\centering\small
\caption{Training configuration. SegFormer follows the encoder/decoder learning-rate recipe of \citet{xie2021segformer}; YOLO follows Ultralytics' own recipe \citep{jocher2026yolo26}. Batch size is not hand-set: a VRAM probe raises it per model up to a cap of 32, which B0 fits and the larger B2 does not. Distillation weights $\alpha$ were selected by Optuna.}
\label{tab:hparams}
\begin{tabular}{lll}
\toprule
Setting & SegFormer & YOLO26n-sem \\
\midrule
Resolution & \multicolumn{2}{c}{$640\times640$} \\
Optimiser & AdamW & auto (Ultralytics) \\
Backbone LR & $6\times10^{-5}$ & --- \\
Decoder LR & $6\times10^{-4}$ ($10\times$) & --- \\
Weight decay & 0.01 & 0.0005 \\
LR schedule & poly, $p{=}1.0$ & cosine, $\mathrm{lr}_f{=}0.01$ \\
Warmup & 1\% of steps & 3 epochs \\
Batch (effective) & 8 (B2) / 32 (B0) & 32 \\
Steps/epoch & 87 (B2) / 21 (B0) & --- \\
Max epochs / patience & \multicolumn{2}{c}{100 / 15} \\
Grad.\ clip & 1.0 & --- \\
AMP & \multicolumn{2}{c}{enabled} \\
Seed & \multicolumn{2}{c}{42} \\
\midrule
Teacher temperature $T$ & \multicolumn{2}{c}{1.840 (NLL-fitted on val)} \\
Distillation $\alpha$ & 0.60 (Optuna) & 0.40 (Optuna) \\
\bottomrule
\end{tabular}
\end{table}

\begin{table}[t]
\centering\small
\caption{Evaluation-protocol ablation. \emph{Identical checkpoints and identical $640\times640$ scoring grid in every row}; only the input pipeline changes. The custom path applies ImageNet normalisation and a stretch-resize; the native path applies Ultralytics' $[0,1]$ scaling and letterbox, matching how the weights were trained. Bypassing a framework's native preprocessing to gain threshold control is not free.}
\label{tab:protocol}
\begin{tabular}{llrrr}
\toprule
Model & Input pipeline & Mean Dice & Med.\ Dice & Miss\% \\
\midrule
\multirow{2}{*}{YOLO-direct}
  & custom (ImageNet norm) & 0.506 & 0.647 & 19.46 \\
  & native (Ultralytics) & 0.735 & 0.849 & 9.73 \\
\midrule
\multirow{2}{*}{YOLO-distilled}
  & custom (ImageNet norm) & 0.608 & 0.726 & 9.73 \\
  & native (Ultralytics) & 0.741 & 0.843 & 7.03 \\
\bottomrule
\end{tabular}
\end{table}
